MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-17-2002 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT, ARGUED, DENY AS TO 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO RELY ON NY LAW, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE WHETHER DEFENDANT BREACHED ITS FIDUCIARY OBLIGATION BY DISCHARGING PLAINTIFF BECAUSE OF HER AGE. SEE GELDER MEDICAL GROUP V. WEBBER, 41 N.Y.2D 680, 684. DENY AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE UCL IS NOT LIMITED TO CONSUMERS AND COMPETITORS. EVEN EMPLOYEES HAVE BEEN ABLE TO MAINTAIN SUCH ACTIONS AGAINST THEIR EMPLOYERS. SEE HUDGINS V. NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC., (1995) 34 CAL.APP.4TH 1109. DENY AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RE OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT AND DEGREE OF DISTRESS. GRANT AS TO 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION. ACTIONS ARE INTENTIONAL. SEE SEMORE VS. POOL 217 CAL.APP.3D 1087, 1105. PREVAIL PARTY TO PREP ORDER. REPORTER: PATRICIA M. EVEZICH, CSR #1193
